findings.title: XSS via innerHTML in dashboard (6 sites)
findings.description: Six innerHTML assignments render unsanitized server-supplied strings directly into the DOM. Task titles, thread names, and agent output are user-controlled data that flow through the harness server and back to this dashboard. Any script tag or event-handler attribute in that data executes in the viewer's browser. The guard confirmed 6 separate innerHTML assignment sites.
findings.action: Replace innerHTML with textContent for plain-text fields. For fields needing HTML structure, construct nodes with createElement/appendChild. If innerHTML is unavoidable, apply DOMPurify.sanitize() before assignment. Audit which fields originate from user-supplied task prompts vs. server-generated metadata.

findings.title: Path traversal in sandbox boundary (12 sites)
findings.description: 12 path-handling sites in harness-sandbox accept caller-supplied path components without canonicalization or boundary verification. The sandbox crate IS the security boundary that prevents spawned agents from escaping their project root. A path traversal here (e.g. '../../../etc/passwd' or symlink-based escape) defeats the entire containment model. This is the most critical path-traversal cluster because it is the last defense before the filesystem.
findings.action: After constructing any path from user input, call std::fs::canonicalize() and assert the result starts_with(allowed_base_dir). Return a permission-denied error if the assertion fails. Add unit tests with '../' sequences and symlink targets confirming they are rejected.

findings.title: Path traversal in GC agent file operations (6 sites)
findings.description: gc_agent.rs performs filesystem operations (worktree cleanup, draft store access) on paths derived from project_root and task metadata without canonicalization. The GC agent is privileged — it deletes worktrees and manages the draft store. A crafted project_root (e.g. '../../sensitive-dir') passed via the API could cause it to delete or read files outside any project boundary. Five additional sites in handlers/gc.rs exhibit the same pattern.
findings.action: Canonicalize all path arguments at the API boundary (HTTP handler / RPC dispatch) before passing them to GC logic. Verify the canonicalized path starts_with a known safe base directory (e.g. the configured data_dir or user home). Reject requests with paths that escape the boundary.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition — deadlock risk in project service (4 sites)
findings.description: Four sites in services/project.rs acquire a second lock while already holding a first. Under concurrent task execution (parallel dispatch is a feature of this codebase), if any caller acquires the same locks in the opposite order a deadlock results. The project service is called from both HTTP handlers and background task runners, increasing inversion probability.
findings.action: Establish a strict global lock acquisition order documented in a comment at the top of AppState. Refactor the four nested-lock sites to acquire locks in canonical order, or restructure so the outer guard is dropped before acquiring the inner lock.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition — deadlock risk in task runner (hot path)
findings.description: task_runner.rs holds a lock during task lifecycle transitions while attempting to acquire a second lock. The task runner is the hot path for all agent execution. A deadlock here blocks every in-flight task. The guard also found dual Task*/Todo* types in this file (RS-12, see F008), suggesting the lock complexity may stem from maintaining two parallel state representations.
findings.action: Audit the nested acquisition site. Drop the outer RwLock guard before acquiring the inner one. Consider whether the Task*/Todo* consolidation (F008) would simplify the locking structure.

findings.title: Architecture violation: direct git CLI calls — unfixed from prior review
findings.description: periodic_reviewer.rs still contains five tokio::process::Command::new("git") calls across gather_repo_structure (line 224), gather_diff_stat (lines 236–263), gather_recent_commits (lines 272–279), and the skip-check (lines 63–76). CLAUDE.md mandates: 'ZERO Command::new("git") calls inside harness crates — all GitHub/git interaction must be in agent prompts only.' The recent commit ff6a343 added three lines to this file without addressing the violation. These calls make the server depend on a git binary in PATH and break the prompt-only delegation model.
findings.action: Move all git context gathering into the review agent prompt: instruct the agent to run 'git ls-files', 'git diff --stat', 'git log --oneline --since=<ts>' as part of its workflow. Remove gather_repo_structure, gather_diff_stat, gather_recent_commits, and the git-log skip-check from periodic_reviewer.rs. Pass only the timestamp of the last review to the prompt.

findings.title: TOCTOU races in DashMap access (exec.rs, workspace.rs, exec_policy.rs)
findings.description: Four DashMap sites across handlers/exec.rs, workspace.rs, and harness-rules/exec_policy.rs use get() + insert() patterns instead of the atomic Entry API. Under concurrent HTTP requests for the same resource, two threads can both see 'not present', both compute the value, and the second write silently overwrites the first. In exec_policy.rs this could allow a policy to be applied twice or have its result clobbered.
findings.action: Replace all get() + insert() patterns with map.entry(key).or_insert_with(|| value) to make the check-and-insert atomic. DashMap's entry API acquires a shard lock that covers both the check and the insert.

findings.title: Dual Task*/Todo* domain model across 7 files
findings.description: Task* and Todo* types coexist in task_runner.rs, task_db.rs, task_queue.rs, intake/mod.rs, harness-core/error.rs, harness-core/agent.rs, and harness-rules/tests/guard_scripts_test.rs. This ambiguity makes it unclear which model is canonical, risks state divergence when only one model is updated, and confuses future contributors about the intended data flow.
findings.action: Audit all Todo* usage. If Todo* maps 1-to-1 to Task*, delete Todo* and update all callsites. If Todo* has a distinct semantic (e.g. external Feishu/Linear todo), document the difference with an explicit comment and a type alias boundary. Either path should leave one authoritative domain model.

findings.title: 24 unwrap() calls in webhook handler — external traffic, high panic risk
findings.description: webhook.rs has the highest unwrap() density in the main codebase (24 sites). Webhook handlers receive external HTTP traffic from GitHub, Feishu, and similar sources. Any malformed payload, unexpected field type, or missing header will panic the handler task. This is the most urgent unwrap cluster because the input is fully external and untrusted.
findings.action: Replace each unwrap() with ? propagation or match/if-let. Return HTTP 400 for client-caused errors (parse failures, missing required fields) and HTTP 500 with tracing::error! for internal errors. Validate the webhook signature before deserializing the body.

findings.title: 20 unwrap() calls in post_validator — validation silently skipped on panic
findings.description: post_validator.rs was recently added and already contains 20 unwrap() calls. Post-validation is a correctness gate that runs after each agent turn. Panics here would abort the validation step silently rather than surfacing an error, potentially allowing incorrect agent output to be accepted without review.
findings.action: Use ? throughout post_validator.rs to propagate errors to the caller. The caller should treat validation errors as soft failures (log + continue) or hard failures (fail the round) based on configured policy, not panic.

findings.title: 33 unwrap() calls in lang_detect — highest absolute count, called per-task
findings.description: lang_detect.rs has 33 unwrap() calls, the highest count in any single file. Most are likely on Regex::new() or string parse operations. This module is invoked on every incoming task prompt to detect language. A regex compilation failure or unexpected input causes an immediate server panic.
findings.action: Compile all regex patterns once at startup using lazy_static or std::sync::OnceLock, so compilation failures surface at boot rather than at request time. Replace runtime parse unwrap() calls with proper error handling or infallible alternatives.

findings.title: 8 silent Result discards in WebSocket layer
findings.description: websocket.rs silently swallows errors at 8 sites using .ok() or .unwrap_or_default(). WebSocket send errors (client disconnect, write back-pressure, frame encoding failure) are dropped with no trace. This makes connection issues completely invisible in production logs.
findings.action: Replace each .ok() with if let Err(e) = ... { tracing::warn!("ws: send error: {e}") }. For sends inside async loops, break or return on error to avoid attempting writes to a closed connection.

findings.title: Hardcoded .db path constructions across 15+ files (80+ violations total)
findings.description: task_db.rs, thread_db.rs, plan_db.rs, project_registry.rs, review_store.rs, router.rs, stdio.rs, scheduler.rs, test_helpers.rs, http.rs, services/execution.rs, services/task.rs, services/project.rs, harness-core/db.rs, and harness-observe/event_store.rs each construct SQLite paths inline. Different entry points can construct different paths for conceptually the same database, fragmenting data across multiple files. harness-core/src/config/dirs.rs already exists but is not used consistently by all callers.
findings.action: Implement or extend a single default_db_path(name: &str) function in harness-core::config::dirs. Update all DB-opening callsites to use it. Guard with an integration test that opens two stores from different callers and verifies they resolve to the same canonical path.

findings.title: Deduplication logic discards updated finding metadata — unfixed from prior review
findings.description: In persist_findings(), when a recurring finding is detected (same rule_id + file), only the review_id is updated in the existing row. The title, description, action, impact, priority, confidence, and effort fields are NOT refreshed. If a finding's severity or fix guidance changes between review runs, the stored record becomes stale and misleads future triage.
findings.action: In the SQL UPDATE branch, update all mutable fields alongside review_id: priority, impact, confidence, effort, title, description, action. Only the original insertion timestamp should be preserved.

findings.title: Concurrent polling spawns still possible despite ff6a343 mitigation
findings.description: Commit ff6a343 added max_rounds=1 and wait_secs=0 to review tasks, shortening their execution window and reducing (but not eliminating) the chance of overlap. The root issue remains: run_review_tick() spawns a polling task via tokio::spawn at line 160 and returns Ok(()) immediately. If the next periodic tick fires while the previous polling task is still running (possible when the review agent takes longer than the tick interval), a second polling task is spawned alongside the first. Over multiple ticks this accumulates background tasks with no bound.
findings.action: Track the active polling JoinHandle in an Arc<Mutex<Option<JoinHandle<()>>>>. In run_review_tick(), check if a handle exists and is still running (handle.is_finished() == false); if so, skip spawning. Alternatively, use a tokio::sync::Semaphore(1) as a review-in-progress guard.
